name: Classical Music
description: Composers, orchestras, and symphonies from Bach to Stravinsky

questions.q: Which Russian composer wrote 'The Nutcracker' and 'Swan Lake'?

source.notes: Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky (1840-1893). Also wrote the 1812 Overture.

questions.q: Tchaikovsky's '1812 Overture' commemorates Russia's victory against which invading force?

source.notes: Includes real cannon fire in performances. Commemorates the Battle of Borodino.

questions.q: Who composed 'The Planets,' a seven-movement orchestral suite with movements named after planets?

source.notes: Gustav Holst. Composed 1914-1917. 'Mars, the Bringer of War' is the most famous movement.

questions.q: Which Austrian composer wrote 'Requiem' while ill, and died before completing it in 1791?

source.notes: Completed posthumously by Franz Xaver Süssmayr. Subject of the film 'Amadeus.'

questions.q: Which nickname was given to Beethoven's famously powerful Piano Concerto No. 5?

source.notes: Not named by Beethoven. In E-flat major. Premiered 1811.

questions.q: A standard string quartet consists of how many musicians?

source.notes: Two violins, one viola, one cello. Pioneered by Haydn.

questions.q: Which musical term means 'very fast'?

source.notes: Above 168 BPM. 'Prestissimo' is even faster (200+).

questions.q: Which slow tempo marking means 'broad' or 'slow'?

source.notes: 40-60 BPM. Italian for 'broad.'

questions.q: Which German composer wrote the Ring Cycle operas held at Bayreuth each year?

source.notes: Richard Wagner. Bayreuth Festival has been held since 1876.

questions.q: Andrea Bocelli is known primarily as what type of musician?

source.notes: Italian tenor, blind since age 12. Known for crossover pop/opera.

questions.q: La Scala, one of the world's most famous opera houses, is located in which city?

source.notes: Opened 1778. Full name: Teatro alla Scala.

questions.q: What is the term for singing without any instrumental accompaniment?

source.notes: Italian for 'in the style of the chapel.' Originally religious vocal music.

questions.q: Which of these instruments is NOT part of the string family?

source.notes: Clarinet is a woodwind. Strings include violin, viola, cello, bass, harp, piano (sometimes).

questions.q: Prokofiev's 'Peter and the Wolf' introduces children to orchestral instruments by assigning them to what?

source.notes: The Bird is flute, the Duck is oboe, the Cat is clarinet, the Wolf is horns.

questions.q: The BBC Proms classical music festival is held annually in which city?

source.notes: Held at Royal Albert Hall. Founded 1895 by Henry Wood. 'Last Night of the Proms' is the famous finale.

questions.q: Who composed the Italian opera 'The Barber of Seville'?

source.notes: Gioachino Rossini, 1816. Famous 'Figaro' aria. Popularized in Bugs Bunny cartoons.

questions.q: Which Baroque composer's most famous work is 'The Four Seasons'?

source.notes: Antonio Vivaldi (1678-1741). Four violin concertos, one per season.

questions.q: Who composed 'Pictures at an Exhibition,' later orchestrated by Ravel?

source.notes: Modest Mussorgsky, 1874. Originally for piano. Inspired by paintings by Viktor Hartmann.

questions.q: Which composer wrote the 'Pastoral' Symphony (No. 6)?

source.notes: Premiered 1808. Depicts rural countryside scenes. Most famous for evoking a storm.

questions.q: Which American composer wrote 'Rhapsody in Blue,' blending jazz and classical music?

source.notes: Composed 1924. Famous opening clarinet glissando. Also wrote 'An American in Paris.'
